'''''UFC 196: McGregor vs. Diaz''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event held on March 5, 2016, at [[MGM Grand Garden Arena]] in [[Las Vegas]], [[Nevada]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2015/09/ufcs-2016-first-quarter-schedule-shapes-up-including-ufc-196-on-feb-6-in-las-vegas|title=UFC's 2016 first-quarter schedule shapes up, including UFC 196 on Feb. 6 in Las Vegas|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2015-09-16|access-date=2016-01-08}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2016/01/report-rafael-dos-anjos-conor-mcgregor-holly-holm-miesha-tate-set-for-ufc-197-in-las-vegas|title=Report: Rafael dos Anjos-Conor McGregor, Holly Holm-Miesha Tate for UFC 197, Las Vegas|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2016-01-07|access-date=2016-01-07}}</ref><ref name="UFC197">{{cite web|url=http://www.ufc.com/news/UFC-197-announced-conor-mcgregor-versus-rafael-dos-anjos-and-holly-holm-vs-miesha-tate|title=It's on! dos Anjos/McGregor, Holm/Tate set for UFC 197 |publisher=ufc.com|author=Thomas Gerbasi|date=2016-01-12|access-date=2016-01-12}}</ref>

==Background==
The event was officially announced in November 2015 as "UFC 197", taking place on March 5, 2016, at [[HSBC Arena (Rio de Janeiro)|HSBC Arena]] in [[Rio de Janeiro]], [[Brazil]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2015/11/ufc-197-set-for-hsbc-arena-in-rio-de-janeiro-on-march-5|title=UFC 197 set for HSBC Arena in Rio de Janeiro on March 5|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2015-11-08|access-date=2016-01-08}}</ref> Weeks later on December 29, [[Ultimate Fighting Championship|UFC]] announced that the event was canceled and would be moved to a different location.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.mmafighting.com/2015/12/29/10684682/ufc-cancels-event-in-rio-de-janeiro-due-to-economic-crisis|title=Anderson Silva: UFC cancels event in Rio de Janeiro|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2015-12-29|access-date=2016-01-08}}</ref> On January 12, it was confirmed the event would take place at the [[MGM Grand Garden Arena]] in [[Las Vegas Valley|Las Vegas]], [[Nevada]], United States.<ref name="UFC197" />

On January 13, UFC revealed this event was originally intended to take place at [[Arena da Baixada]] (a much larger venue) in [[Curitiba]], [[Paraná (state)|Paraná]], [[Brazil]], and feature a [[UFC Heavyweight Championship]] rematch between champion [[Fabrício Werdum]] and former two-time champion [[Cain Velasquez]], with former [[UFC Middleweight Championship|UFC Middleweight Champion]] [[Anderson Silva]] in the co-main event slot, possibly against [[Michael Bisping]]. Due to a series of injuries, the company moved the heavyweight bout to a separate event scheduled for February 6, which at that time was [[UFC Fight Night: Hendricks vs. Thompson|titled "UFC 196"]]. This change made the March 5 event a smaller draw, which led to the transition to the smaller venue in [[Rio de Janeiro]]. To maintain local interest, the company then considered booking the main event as a rematch between two [[Brazilians|Brazilian]] fighters, Silva and former [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship|UFC Light Heavyweight Champion]] [[Vitor Belfort]]. Belfort declined the bout, as he wished to wait for the result of the [[Middleweight (MMA)|middleweight]] title fight at [[UFC 194]]. This final fall-through is what prompted the cancellation of the event in Brazil and relocation to [[Las Vegas]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.mmafighting.com/2016/1/13/10761276/ufc-official-confirms-vitor-belfort-turned-down-fight-with-anderson|title=UFC official confirms Vitor Belfort turned down fight with Anderson Silva at UFC 197|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2016-01-13|access-date=2016-01-13}}</ref> On January 27, it was revealed the Werdum-Velasquez bout (which had been moved to "UFC 196" scheduled for February 6, 2016) was cancelled entirely. This forced that event to alter into a "UFC Fight Night" card, and the originally scheduled "UFC 197" event was renamed UFC 196.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.ufc.com/news/Hendricks-Thompson-headlines-UFC-Fight-Night|title=Hendricks-Thompson headlines UFC Fight Night|publisher=ufc.com|author=Staff|date=2016-01-27|access-date=2016-01-27}}</ref>

The event was expected to be headlined by a [[UFC Lightweight Championship]] bout between champion [[Rafael dos Anjos]] and [[UFC Featherweight Championship|UFC Featherweight Champion]] [[Conor McGregor]].<ref name="UFC197" /> This would have been the second time in [[Ultimate Fighting Championship|UFC]] history that champions in different divisions would fight for the same title. The first time was at [[UFC 94]] on January 31, 2009, when then-[[UFC Welterweight Championship|UFC Welterweight Champion]] [[Georges St-Pierre]] defended his title against then-lightweight champion [[B.J. Penn]]. On February 23, the planned bout suffered a setback as it was announced that dos Anjos pulled out due to a broken foot.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.mmafighting.com/2016/2/23/11098398/rafael-dos-anjos-injured-out-of-ufc-196-main-event-fight-against|title=Rafael dos Anjos injured, out of UFC 196 main event fight against Conor McGregor|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Ariel Helwani|date=2016-02-23|access-date=2016-02-23}}</ref> Former [[UFC Featherweight Championship|UFC Featherweight Champion]] [[José Aldo]] declined the initial opportunity to replace him due to "lack of time" to prepare for the bout.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.mmafighting.com/2016/2/23/11099670/coach-jose-aldo-not-in-shape-turned-down-offer-to-replace-rafael-dos|title=Coach: Jose Aldo not in shape, turned down offer to replace Rafael dos Anjos at UFC 196|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2016-02-23|access-date=2016-02-23}}</ref> Former lightweight champion and top featherweight contender [[Frankie Edgar]] also declined the opportunity due to a groin injury.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.mmafighting.com/2016/2/24/11105514/frankie-edgar-pissed-at-ufc-says-the-c-in-ufc-stands-for-conor|title=Frankie Edgar 'pissed' at UFC, says the 'C' in UFC stands for 'Conor'|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Chuck Mindenhall|date=2016-02-24|access-date=2016-02-24}}</ref> Eventually, [[The Ultimate Fighter 5]] winner and former lightweight title challenger [[Nate Diaz]] was announced as the replacement and the bout took place in the welterweight division.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.foxsports.com/ufc/story/ufc-196-conor-mcgregor-nate-diaz-new-main-event-022316|title=Nate Diaz gets the call, faces Conor McGregor in new UFC 196 main event|work=foxsports.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2016-02-23|access-date=2016-02-23}}</ref>

A [[UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship]] bout between then champion [[Holly Holm]] and former [[Strikeforce Women's Bantamweight Championship|Strikeforce Women's Bantamweight Champion]] [[Miesha Tate]] co-headlined the event.<ref name="UFC197" />

[[Michael Johnson (fighter)|Michael Johnson]] was expected to face [[Tony Ferguson]] in a lightweight rematch. On January 27, it was announced that Johnson withdrew from the bout due to injury. Ferguson was then rescheduled to fight at [[UFC on Fox: Teixeira vs. Evans|UFC on Fox 19]] on April 16 against [[Khabib Nurmagomedov]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.mmafighting.com/2016/1/26/10838404/khabib-nurmagomedov-vs-tony-ferguson-targeted-for-april-ufc-on-fox?_ga=1.211413789.94603769.1452949280|title=Khabib Nurmagomedov vs. Tony Ferguson targeted for April UFC on Fox event|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Hunter A. Homistek|date=2016-01-26|access-date=2016-01-27}}</ref>

A welterweight bout between [[Brandon Thatch]] and [[Siyar Bahadurzada]], originally slated for [[UFC Fight Night: Cowboy vs. Cowboy]], was rescheduled to this event due to Thatch's medication being banned by Pennsylvania's athletic commission.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2016/02/medication-clearance-issue-moves-siyar-bahadurzada-vs-brandon-thatch-to-ufc-196|title='Medication' clearance issue moves Siyar Bahadurzada vs. Brandon Thatch to UFC 196|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2016-02-12|access-date=2016-02-12}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters were awarded $50,000 bonuses:<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sherdog.com/news/news/UFC-196-Bonuses-Nate-Diaz-Takes-Home-36100K-for-Upset-Victory-Over-Conor-McGregor-101389|title=UFC 196 Bonuses: Nate Diaz takes home $100K for upset victory over Conor McGregor|publisher=sherdog.com|author= Tristen Critchfield|access-date=2016-03-06|date=2016-03-06}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Conor McGregor vs. Nate Diaz'''
*'''Performance of the Night:''' '''Nate Diaz''' and '''Miesha Tate'''

==Reported payout==
The following is the reported payout to the fighters as reported to the [[Nevada Athletic Commission|Nevada State Athletic Commission]]. It does not include sponsor money and also does not include the UFC's traditional "fight night" bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sherdog.com/news/news/UFC-196-Salaries-Conor-McGregor-Earns-361-Million-Nate-Diaz-Holly-Holm-Pocket-36500K-101387|title=UFC 196 salaries: Conor McGregor earns $1 Million; Nate Diaz, Holly Holm pocket $500K|publisher=sherdog.com|date=2016-03-06|access-date=2016-03-06|author=Staff}}</ref>

* '''Nate Diaz:''' $500,000 (no win bonus) def. Conor McGregor: $1,000,000
* '''Miesha Tate:''' $92,000 (includes $46,000 win bonus) def. Holly Holm: $500,000
* '''Ilir Latifi:''' $50,000 (includes $25,000 win bonus) def. Gian Villante: $36,000
* '''Corey Anderson:''' $40,000 (includes $20,000 win bonus) def. Tom Lawlor: $28,000
* '''Amanda Nunes:''' $56,000 (includes $28,000 win bonus) def. Valentina Shevchenko: $14,000
* '''Siyar Bahadurzada:''' $38,000 (includes $19,000 win bonus) def. Brandon Thatch: $22,000
* '''Nordine Taleb:''' $30,000 (includes $15,000 win bonus) def. Erick Silva: $34,000
* '''Vitor Miranda:''' $32,000 (includes $16,000 win bonus) def. Marcelo Guimarães: $13,000
* '''Darren Elkins:''' $74,000 (includes $37,000 win bonus) def. Chas Skelly: $21,000
* '''Diego Sanchez:''' $150,000 (includes $75,000 win bonus) def. Jim Miller: $59,000
* '''Jason Saggo:''' $20,000 (includes $10,000 win bonus) def. Justin Salas: $16,000
* '''Teruto Ishihara:''' $34,000 (includes $17,000 win bonus) def. Julian Erosa: $12,000
